title: Beloperone carthaginensis
intro: Beloperone carthaginensis, also known as cat's claw vine or trumpet vine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, thanks to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Beloperone carthaginensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into supplements and capsules.
taxonomy: Beloperone carthaginensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Beloperone. The Acanthaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or acanthus family. The family Acanthaceae is part of the order Lamiales, which is a large order of flowering plants. These plants are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions.

The flavonoids present in Beloperone carthaginensis, such as kaempferol and quercetin, are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
